text.title: Prompt Engineering — Production Patterns — Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules that gate any application of the prompt-engineering-production methodology. Sources: Anthropic prompt-engineering documentation; OpenAI prompt-engineering production guide; Anthropic prompt-caching docs.

rule.statement: Pin exact model version + prompt hash at every gateway call; never let either roll silently.
rule.rationale: Unpinned versions cause silent regressions whose root cause appears days after the fact.

rule.statement: Structure prompts with the long, stable prefix first (system, context, examples) and the variable part last; this maximises Anthropic / OpenAI prompt-cache hit rate.
rule.rationale: Cache hits cut latency 2–10× and cost 90%; cache miss patterns are a function of prompt structure, not luck.

rule.statement: Define explicit fallback per prompt: degrade to a smaller model on timeout, return a canned response on repeated failure, never let the system hang.
rule.rationale: Without fallback, a 10× latency spike at the provider becomes a 10× latency spike for customers; fallback is the only mitigation.

rule.statement: Wrap user-supplied content in clearly-delimited blocks (XML tags); never concatenate user text into the system prompt.
rule.rationale: Concatenated user text becomes system instruction; clearly-delimited blocks plus instruction-hierarchy prompts close the most common injection vectors.

rule.statement: Emit telemetry per call: prompt id, prompt hash, model id, latency, tokens, cost, finish_reason, eval-suite id; route to your observability platform.
rule.rationale: Telemetry is the only mechanism that lets you correlate prompt regressions to specific changes after the fact.
